After reviewing the retrieved materials, none of the documents mention SATB1 expression levels or genetic profiles in either normal thymocytes or thymoma tissues. While several sources reference key immunophenotypic markers like CD3, CD4, CD8, TdT, and others used to identify thymic cell subsets—including double-negative and double-positive thymocytes—the absence of any citation concerning SATB1 suggests there is insufficient current evidence to confirm its expression in thymocytes. Therefore, despite earlier assumptions grounded in general biological functions, specific experimental validation remains missing. Hence, I conclude that the available data do not support affirming SATB1 expression in thymocytes.
